data.title: orchestrator uses generic Agent instead of named dso:code-reviewer-* sub-agent type in REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md dispatch
data.description: The REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md Step 4 dispatch specifies that the orchestrator MUST use named dso:code-reviewer-* agents via the Agent tool with subagent_type='general-purpose' and the agent file content verbatim. However the orchestrator (Claude) dispatches a generic Agent without loading the named agent file content or using the correct subagent_type. This bypasses the review contract and constitutes the fabrication the workflow explicitly prohibits. The REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md Step 4 states: 'Dispatching a generic agent with instructions to write reviewer-findings.json is also fabrication — the review MUST come from a named reviewer agent.' The orchestrator is using a generic Agent call with a manually constructed prompt instead of reading the agent file and using its content verbatim as required.
